title: MCP Architecture, Negotiation, and Trust Boundaries · Trigger Forensics
summary: Trace one tool call across host, client, server, and external system. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish host, per-server client, server, model, user, and external service responsibilities.
Trace initialization, capability negotiation, requests, responses, notifications, and errors.
Advertise and negotiate only implemented protocol capabilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Spec Versioning evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Spec Versioning assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use a changed debug case for agent ext mcp architecture that tests this competency: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Order work so Spec Versioning has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.
